description_long: This is a ceramic vase with a wide, rounded base and a slightly flared cylindrical upper section, featuring a single, thick handle attached to the side. The rim is uneven and chipped, suggesting age or wear, and the surface is adorned with hand-painted green and grayish motifs, including swirling lines and dotted bands. The body of the vase is primarily a pale beige or light tan color, with decorative bands encircling both the upper and lower portions. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the body, providing both functional and aesthetic value. The surface finish is matte with visible textural irregularities, indicating it was likely hand-crafted. This vase would be suitable as a decorative object in an urban park, public square, or as part of a street-side installation.
description: A beige ceramic vase with a wide base, flared rim, and a single thick handle, decorated with green and gray painted motifs and visible surface wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handle on the right and the most complete section of the painted motifs, with the chipped rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the handle in full profile, highlighting the curvature and attachment point, and a clear view of the painted band around the base.
description_view_2: Rear angled view with the handle on the left, showing more of the vase's back and the continuation of the decorative motifs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle in partial profile, emphasizing the uneven rim and the painted patterns wrapping around the body.
